Corydalis is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the poppy family (Papaveraceae). The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, depression, and insomnia, due to its sedative and anxiolytic properties. The bioactive constituents of Corydalis include alkaloids such as tetrahydropalmatine, palmatine, and corydaline, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Corydalis is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as capsules, tablets, and teas, which are often used to treat stress-related disorders and promote relaxation.
